prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis is an ongoing economic crisis and part of 2022-2023 political unrest in Pakistan. It has caused severe economic challenges for months due to which food, gas and oil prices have risen. The Russian invasion of Ukraine has caused fuel prices to rise worldwide. Excessive external borrowings by the country over the years raised the spectre of default, causing the currency to fall and making imports more expensive in relative terms. By June 2022, inflation was at an all time high, along with rising food prices.Poor governance and low productivity per capita in comparison with other low to middle-income developing countries have contributed to a balance of payment crisis, where the country is unable to earn enough foreign exchange to fund the imports that
B. Pakistan has been experiencing an ongoing economic downturn, known as the 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis, which is intrinsically linked to the 2022-2023 political instability within the nation. This situation has led to significant economic difficulties over several months, resulting in increased costs for essential goods such as food, gas, and oil. The global increase in fuel prices has been exacerbated by the Russian invasion of Ukraine. A history of substantial external borrowing by the country has created the risk of a financial default, leading to a depreciation of its currency and, consequently, making imported goods more costly when compared to domestic alternatives. By June 2022, the rate of inflation had reached an unprecedented peak, accompanied by a simultaneous surge in food prices. Contributing factors to this balance of payments crisis include substandard governance and a comparatively low level of productivity per person when contrasted with other developing nations in the low to middle-income bracket. This crisis manifests as the country's inability to generate sufficient foreign currency to finance its import needs.
C. The Pakistan economic crisis of 2022-2023 is a result of ongoing economic challenges and political turmoil in Pakistan. It has caused significant economic problems for several months, including a rise in food, gas, and oil prices, a worldwide currency shock from the Russian invasion of Ukraine, and a risk of default. Inflation was at an all-time high, along with high inflation rates.

Answer:

options.A: The 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis is an ongoing economic crisis and part of 2022-2023 political unrest in Pakistan. It has caused severe economic challenges for months due to which food, gas and oil prices have risen. The Russian invasion of Ukraine has caused fuel prices to rise worldwide. Excessive external borrowings by the country over the years raised the spectre of default, causing the currency to fall and making imports more expensive in relative terms. By June 2022, inflation was at an all time high, along with rising food prices.Poor governance and low productivity per capita in comparison with other low to middle-income developing countries have contributed to a balance of payment crisis, where the country is unable to earn enough foreign exchange to fund the imports that
options.B: Pakistan has been experiencing an ongoing economic downturn, known as the 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis, which is intrinsically linked to the 2022-2023 political instability within the nation. This situation has led to significant economic difficulties over several months, resulting in increased costs for essential goods such as food, gas, and oil. The global increase in fuel prices has been exacerbated by the Russian invasion of Ukraine. A history of substantial external borrowing by the country has created the risk of a financial default, leading to a depreciation of its currency and, consequently, making imported goods more costly when compared to domestic alternatives. By June 2022, the rate of inflation had reached an unprecedented peak, accompanied by a simultaneous surge in food prices. Contributing factors to this balance of payments crisis include substandard governance and a comparatively low level of productivity per person when contrasted with other developing nations in the low to middle-income bracket. This crisis manifests as the country's inability to generate sufficient foreign currency to finance its import needs.
options.C: The Pakistan economic crisis of 2022-2023 is a result of ongoing economic challenges and political turmoil in Pakistan. It has caused significant economic problems for several months, including a rise in food, gas, and oil prices, a worldwide currency shock from the Russian invasion of Ukraine, and a risk of default. Inflation was at an all-time high, along with high inflation rates.
